properties.remark: Rockslide reported along KY-160 across from the Golden Apple Fruit Market.
Poor drainage flooding reported along Perkins Madden Road, especially near the entrance to the Knott County Nursing Home.
All lanes on KY 1780 at mile point 11.5 in Leslie County are currently blocked due to a mudslide.

A weather service employee observed what he estimated to be at least 6 inches of water flowing across Highway 460 at 11695 Main St in Jeffersonville, KY just to the west of the Menifee/Montgomery county line in Montgomery county.
A weather service employee called to report that water from Brush Creek was flowing over Main Street near the car lot in Clay City near exit 16.
A member of the Lexington, KY news media posted a picture on Facebook of flooding near Clay City. Water was completely covering the Mountain Parkway near the Clay City overpass.

Water was observed flowing across Hwy 1274 and Main St in Frenchburg and also over Hwy 460 in Means. These roads were not completely closed, but the Menifee County Highway Dept did have to put up barricades to keep folks from driving into the flooded parts of the roads.
KY-1460 was closed due to rockslide across from the Pikeville Elementary School.

Delayed, Post-Survey Report. Multiple trees were snapped along Big Cave Road (KY-1004), with their trunks blown uniformly to the south. This appears to be related to a Rear Flank Downdraft surge ahead of tornado genesis. Scattered tree damage started at the intersection of Big Cave Road and Salt Peter Road. The straight-line wind damage ended at Red Hill Road (KY-1955).
TOR Possible. Report of a roof off a house on Red Hill Road in Livingston, KY. Individual contacted the County Emergency Manager about a possible tornado. Time estimated via radar.
The National Weather Service confirms EF-1 tornado damage in the Robinet Community, on the eastern side of Rockcastle County, via storm survey. Time estimated via radar.
The National Weather Service confirms EF-1 tornado damage near the Island City Community, on the southwestern side of Owsley County, via storm survey. Time estimated via radar.
TOR possible. Delayed report relayed via broadcast media. Photographs of structural damage and downed trees in between Sextons Creek and Island City. Time estimated via radar.

Corrects previous tornado report from 1 SE Island City. The National Weather Service confirms EF-1 tornado damage near the Island City Community, on the southwestern side of Owsley County, via storm survey. Time estimated via radar.

Corrects previous tornado report from 1 SE Island City. The National Weather Service confirms EF-1 tornado damage near the Island City Community, on the southwestern side of Owsley County, via storm survey. Time estimated via radar.
Corrects previous report from 2 SE Wendover. Tree blown down in the 1800 block of Camp Creek Road.

Abandoned trailer home destroyed by thunderstorm winds. Time estimated as approximately the same time as the Whitesburg, KY Mesonet reported a 53 mph gust. The mesonet station is approximately 160 to 170 feet from the site of the destroyed home.
Rockslide blocked both lanes of KY-3 around MM 4.0.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 SSW Wasioto. Tree fell on a power line on road 190. Tree fell at intersection of road 190 and Beckys Branch Road. Time was Also adjusted based on radar.
NWS Morristown relayed a public report of golf ball sized hail in Cumberland. Time estimated via radar.
Tree fell on a power line on road 190.
Report of damage along Madison Avenue in Corbin. Large tree was uprooted and fell on a residence and a vehicle. Time estimated via radar.

Updates previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 E Corbin. Report of damage along Madison Avenue in Corbin. Large tree was uprooted and fell on a residence and a vehicle. Tree was blown eastward, with no other damage nearby. Time estimated via radar.
Delayed report of a row of uprooted trees blown uniformly eastward onto a fence behind a residence on Elm Street in Corbin.
Delayed report of a line of trees down behind a residence on John Street in Corbin. Trees were blown in a uniform direction consistent with other straight line wind damage nearby.
Multiple trees reported down along US-421 between One Stop Gas station and Stinnett Hill Road.
Tree fell over Prewitt Bend Rd, about 1.5 miles off US-25. Time estimated from radar.
Tor Possible. Leslie County 911 Center reports many tree limbs and other debris scattered around the intersection of 421 and 2058. Time estimated via radar.

Updates previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 NW Bledsoe. Leslie County 911 Center previously reported many tree limbs downed along 2058 before the intersection with 421. This report was confirmed by an NWS Storm Survey team, with scattered tree damage observed between 1780 and 421. Time estimated via radar.
Quarter sized hail reported at Big Branch Hill off of lick fork of Beefhide. Time estimated via radar.
Delayed Report. NWS Storm Survey team discovered many tree branches snapped along KY-221 in between 421 and 2009 in NW Harlan County. Damage was unidirectional. Time estimated via radar.
US-23 MM 19-20 NB lane was closed due to rockfall.
A tree was reportedly blown down onto KY-38 near MM 28. Time estimated.
All lanes of KY 11 North (MP 18.411; Green Briar Hollow Road) were blocked due to a rock slide.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 2 NNW Dorthae. Pickup truck became trapped in flash flood water at the intersection of E KY-552 and Cooper Hill Lane. Road was closed. Water depth was estimated to around 3.5 feet. Location is typically not prone to flooding.
KY-15 was shut down between the KY-160 caution lights and the top of Red Oak Mountain due to multiple rock slides and water over the roadway.
US-421 was temporarily closed around One Stop Gas Station due to approximately 3 feet of water flowing over the highway.

Pickup truck became trapped in flash flood water at the intersection of E KY-552 and Cooper Hill Lane. Road was closed. Water depth was estimated to around 3.5 feet. Location is typically not prone to flooding.
Flash flood waters inundated multiple structures at the mouth of the Red Oak Branch near the intersection of Red Oak Branch Road and KY-15.
Driveways washed out, water in basements of multiple homes.
Landslide reported on Main Street in Hazard.
Landslide/rockslide across KY 2446 Lost Creek-Dice Road blocking at least half of the roadway. Via photos and report on social media.

Landslide/rockslide with downed trees across KY Rte 1426 Town Mountain Rd at mile marker 7.9 near Williamson Hollow Rd. Road was closed for several hours overnight, but has reopened. Via KYTC District 12 Facebook page.
Report on social media showed flash flooding across Elisha Feltner Road, with Laurel River flowing out of its banks and across the road, making the road impassable and flooding the nearby fields.
Public report of 6 inches of rushing watch across S. KY-233 in Gray, Kentucky. Time based off time of call.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding; Street/road closed; Vehicles stranded.
At least 6 inches of water across KY Highway 1526 in Knox County. Water is running down the street. Photos provided via social media.
Social media report showing flowing water going across Sam Parker Road (1061 Left Hazel Fork Road) making it impassable. Other locations along Sam Parker Road are also flooding.

Social media report shared by Lexington media showing flooding across Master Street near the Wentworth Street intersection. A truck got swept up in the flood waters and landed in a ditch across from the Sav-Rite family pharmacy. Road is now blocked off by police.
Social media report of water rushing across the road on KY-66 in Arjay just past Mills Furniture and Appliance at the mouth of Wiser Branch.
Public reported water over the roadway at Hwy 204 West and Davenport Lane in Williamsburg Ky.
Highway 490 was flooded due to Hazel Patch Creek being out of its banks and flowing across the road just north of East Bernstadt.
Social Media Photographs of water overtopping Locust Grove Road in Keavy, Laurel County.

Pictures on social media showed Dry Fork out of its banks and running across Highway 3401 at the 180 block, making it impassable. It was also infringing on properties on the southeast side of the creek.
A picture on social media shows a mudslide due to heavy rain blocking much of KY-805 just northeast of Martha Jane Potter Elementary (690 block).
Corrects previous flood report from 2 ENE Co Operative. KY Highway 1363 closed at MM8 due to water across the road.
Video on social media of water flowing over Corinth Cemetery Road S of Corbin.

Picture from a residence at 3537 Millstone Road (about half a mile north of Millstone Missionary Baptist Church) showed Millstone Creek out of its banks and flowing over the road, making the road impassable.

A social media post showed KY-160 under swift moving water just south of Lincoln Drive due to water out of the banks on the west side of the road. The posted picture showed a car stuck in the flood waters on the road.

Radio station in Bell County reported that crews were on their way to conduct a swift water rescue for a a stranded motorist that tried to cross a water-covered roadway on Highway 221 (mile marker 9) in Stoney Fork.
Multiple pictures coming in showing portions of the downtown Neon area flooding, impacting buildings and causing roads to be impassable.

A social media post on a Floyd Count Radio Station Facebook page showed impactful flash flooding going across State Hwy 931 around the Cowan Elementary School, including flowing water that is surrounding the school and neighboring buildings in the area.

Letcher County Sheriffs Office relayed pictures on social media at the 800 block of Crafts Colley Road showing Crafts Colley Creek well out of its banks and flowing across the road, making the road impassable.

A social media report showed video of water as it was rising out of the banks of Troublesome Creek and across KY-550 making it impassable near the 1230 block. The water came up at least a foot across the road in the matter of 40 minutes accoding to the videos posted.
KY Transportation Cabinet District 11 social media page reports landslide/mudslide across KY Highway 522 at MM 1.8. Road is estimated to be closed about 5 hours.

A residence at 10047 KY-160 (Whitesburg) shared a picture on social media showing the creek along the road out of its banks and flowing down the road making it impassable. The video also showed a stranded motorist on top of their car in the middle of the flood waters.
Trained spotter reported that KY-4028 and KY-38 is underwater in Evarts, KY.
Several inches of water was flowing across a road in Busy Kentucky.
KY Transportation Cabinet District 11 reports a landslide/rockslide covering KY Highway 257 at MM 2. Road is closed. Report via their social media page.
Mudslide has closed KY Highway 225 between mile markers 4 and 5. Report via the KY Transportation Cabinet District 11 Facebook page.
Corrects previous flood report from 1 NW Jarvis. KY Highway 229 at mile marker 4.5 closed due to a mudslide/rockslide. Report via the KY Transportation Cabinet District 11 Facebook page.
Trained spotter reported 2.20 inches of rain has fallen since 7 AM and there is rushing water near his house.
Video posted on social media of Branhams Creek flooding onto KY Highway 3379 near Galveston. Location estimated via social media report. Creek runs beside the road for many miles.

Kentucky Transportation Cabinet District 10 posted a picture of KY-451 in the Krypton with high flood waters (in the 2800 block). A car was stranded in the middle of the road and needed rescuing, with pictures showing the water over halfway up the sides of the car.
Part of the hillside slid onto the KY-931, just below Bass Road north of Whitesburg.
Video shared on social media shows the creek/river out of its banks and flooding W Main St in Hindman. Time estimated.
Much of downtown Jenkins is experiencing flash flooding due to Elkhorn Creek rising out of its banks and flowing through the town. The water is threatening buildings throughout the downtown area.

Pictures on social media showed water flowing out of the creek (Yonts Fork) and up and down Yonts Fork Road (500 block of Yonts Fork near near the intersection with Kodia Road), at least 6 inches deep in many locations, making the road impassible.
Portion of the road has collapsed from flood waters at KY Highway 1087 at mile marker 4.0.
KYTC photographs of downed trees and washed out lane along KY-1087 between MP 4 and 5.
Left Beaver Creek is out of its banks near McDowell First Baptist Church and flooding side streets.
KY Highway 1469/Penny Rd is impassable due to high water near US Highway 23. Report via KY Transportation Cabinet District 12 Facebook page. Additional social media posts as well.
Video shared by local media on social media of water from Fighting Creek moving swiftly across KY Highway 3439 and surrounding houses in Barbourville.
Photo of water from Steele Creek over KY-1086. The road is impassable.
NWS Charleston WV relayed a public report they received about Elkhorn Creek overflowing its banks and covering Hwy 197 between Ashcamp and Big Branch. The road is impassable due to water.
Photo shared via social media showing swift water moving across KY Highway 3401 at Tyler Lane.

A Clay County man drove into water covering KY-80 in the Horse Creek Community of Clay County, near Manchester. The man got out of his vehicle, and the current swept him several hundred feet according to the coroner said. The coroners office received the call to respond to the scne at 5:25pm EST, however flooded routes made it hard for emergency workers to access him. The body was recovered and the man was pronounced dead at 7:30pm.
Water moving swiftly across KY Highway 1098 at Smith Branch. Report with photo via social media. Time estimated.
KY Transportation Cabinet District 10 shared a photo on social media of mudslide across KY Highway 267 at MP 5.5. Road is blocked.
A significant mudslide occurred 3 miles up Middle Fork Rd, off US 23 at Hagerhill. Entire road is blocked. Road closed through tonight.
Power lines down at the end of Dreyfus Rd going onto Clark Rd and Knob Lick in Estill County. Time estimated by radar.
Trees and powerlines were downed with a strong line of thunderstorms near the intersection of 8th St. and Hillside Dr in Corbin. Time is estimated by radar.
Trees and power lines were downed at the intersection of 5th street and Scuffletown Road. Time was estimated based on radar.
AWOS station KI35 Harlan Apt.
Social media report of a roof blown off Bombshell Salon and Spa building on the 900 block of Broadway St. in Paintsville. Time estimate from radar.
A new mudslide this morning covered both sides of KY-931 South on the Kingscreek side of Cowan Mountain (just east of Larry Road on the west side of the bend).
Mesonet station DORT Pikeville 13 S.
Social media report of a tree down on Rice Station Rd. Time estimated from radar.
Powerlines were reported to have been blown down at 650 Dry Creek Road Clearfield. Time is estimated from radar.
Powerlines were reported to have been blown down at 2966 Dry Creek Road Clearfield. Time is estimated from radar.
Social media report of several trees down on Cow Creek Road. Time estimated from radar.
Social media report of storm damage to a roof on the 700 block of Lowes Branch Road. Time from social media post.
Pulaski County Dispatch reported a downed tree on a powerline on the 450 Block of Sierra Dr near Burnside. Time is based off of time of 911 call.
Pulaski County 911 Center reported several trees down at the intersection of Malvin Hill Church Road and Jessie Sears Road. Time estimated from radar.
Pulaski County 911 Center reported a tree down near the 10000 Block of KY 192. Time estimated from radar.
Pulaski County 911 Center reported a tree down near the intersection KY 192 and Providence Road. Time estimated from radar.
Report including video via social media.
A tree was reported to have been blown down near the intersection of Charlie Sizemore Road and US 421. The event time is radar estimated.
Perry County 911 Center reported utility lines down near the 2700 Block of KY-28 near the Grapevine area. Time estimated from radar.
Perry County 911 Center reported a tree down on KY-451 before Messer Branch Road. Time estimated from radar.
Public report relayed by broadcast from Valley Road of several trees that have blown down and blocked the road.

Straight line wind damage was observed along the hillside near Brush Creek at the intersection of Highway 550 and Highway 850, with several trees uprooted or snapped heading in a northeasterly direction. Peak winds likely between 90 and 100 mph.

Straight line wind damage was observed at a residence, north of the intersection of Highway 850 and 550, where wind hit the broad side of a barn before peeling the roof off. Several trees were also snapped or uprooted. A 300 year old oak tree also sustained damage. Peak winds likely between 90 and 100 mph.
Damage observed from photos near the town of Langley, in Floyd County. Time estimated from radar.
Social media report said trees and powerlines were down on the 120 block of Turner Branch Road in Eastern.

Significant damage was observed at Floyd County Board of Education center, where wind penetrated the steel doors, before escaping through the NW portion of the gymnasium roof, blowing out a substantial section.
Trees and powerlines were reported to have been blown down in the Printer community. The event time is radar estimated.
Trees and powerlines were reported to have been blown down in the Beaver community near KY 979. The event time is radar estimated.
Considerable damage off of US-23 in Broad Bottom. Time estimated by radar.

A roof was blown off a structure with additional damage to that structures second story with debris reported to be blocking all lanes of US 23. Powerlines were also blown down at this location and also nearby on Ziegler Road. The event time is radar estimated.

An outer wall collapsed and significant roof damage was sustained at an older office building between College Boulevard and Sunset drive south of Highway 23. Debris was blown downwind towards Highway 23, with a semi-trailer just north of Highway 23 being pivoted by the wind in a similar direction.
Sporadic tree damage was noted along Red Bird Lane, with additional tree damage having occurred across the Levisa Fork Big Sandy River.
Powerlines were reported to have been blown down at the mouth of Long Fork. The event time is radar estimated.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 SW Piso. Social media report of storm damage to a roof on the 700 block of Lowes Branch Road. Time from social media post.
Fleming County 911 relayed a report of a down tree at the corner of Harn Road and Turner Road. Time estimated from radar.

Emergency manager reported a tree on a house at 905 Furnace Jct, northeast of Ravenna. The home is badly damaged as a result. There are other reports of trees down on roads throughout the county from the original round of storms that moved through at approximately 3:35am.

A large tree was downed at the intersection of Red Lick Road and Coral Avenue in the community of Wisemantown. The tree took some power or phone lines down with it, which are now blocking most of the road.
A tree was reported downed on a house at 2375 Cow Creek Road (house to the east of this address) in the Willow Creek Community. Time estimated by radar.
A barn collapsed at 910 Cane Creek Road (State Hwy 615/N. Bend Road) due to thunderstorm winds. Time estimated by radar.
A barn located at 1450 South Fork Road received considerable roof damage, along with wood siding being ripped off of one side. Time estimated from radar.

A house on the south side of Campton Road (just east of the intersection with Deer Trace) suffered considerable roof damage due to severe thunderstorm winds. Multiple trees on the property were also felled. Heavy rain on top of the roof damage eventually caused a partial roof collapse inside the home.
Rowan County 911 Center reported a tree down on the 500 block of Crix Ridge Rd. Time estimated from radar.
Mesonet station WLBT West Liberty 2 SW.
Rockcastle County 911 Center reported a tree down on 100 block of Upper Calloway Loop near the intersection of S. Wilderness Road. Time estimated from radar.
Johnson County 911 Center relayed a report of a down tree on KY-581 in Tutor Key. Time estimated from radar.
Pulaski County 911 Center reported a tree blocking both lanes near the 1000 block of Pea Ridge Road. Time estimated from radar.
Jackson County 911 Center reported a tree down on 5000 block of KY 587, near New Zion Cemetery. Time estimated from radar.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 E Bent. Pulaski County 911 Center reported a tree blocking both lanes near the 1000 block of Pea Ridge Road. Time estimated from radar.
Rockcastle 911 Center reported several trees down on South Wilderness Road, 2 miles south of Livingston near the Laurel County line. Time estimated from radar.
Clay County Highway Garage reported a tree down in Sextons Creek 1 mile NE from the intersection of KY-577 and KY-1350. Time estimated from radar.
Water flowing over part of Centerville Rd. Picture on social media.
Montgomery County EM reported that Main St and adjoining streets are under water. They are active evacuating two apartment complexes.
Creek is out of banks and inundating homes near Creekside Drive along Abbott Creek. Video on social media. Time estimated by radar.
Creek out of its banks and water flowing around homes on Middle Fork Davis Branch Rd. Video on social media. Time estimated by radar.
Flood waters overtopped a bridge at the intersection of Highway 30 and 1090. Pictures on social media. Time estimated by radar.
Water flowing across parts of Fairview Loop Road. Pictures on social media. Time estimated by radar.
Creek out of banks and water flowing around several homes on Silk Stocking Loop. Pictures from social media. Time estimated by radar.
Water flowing across Morgue Fork Rd. Pictures on social media.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 4 N Perkins. The Whitley County EM confirmed that they received a call about a tree on vehicle with entrapment. However, there was no evidence of this incident when responders got to the scene, and there are no known entrapments.
Trees down with a tree on vehicle.
Delayed social media report (w/ pictures) of snapped tree trunks and scattered downed branches along Eaton Fork Road in Knox County. Time estimated via radar.

Whitley County EM reports localized straight line wind damage along Mosley Branch Road headed towards Knox County, just to the NE of the intersection with 1064. Outbuildings were damaged along with downed trees and power lines.
Scattered tree damage along Mosley Branch Rd in Knox County. Time estimated by radar.
Clay County State Garage reported 2 trees down 1 mile from the US 421-KY 638 intersection on KY 638. Time estimated from radar.
Two large trees were reported to have been blow down along KY 834 near the intersection with Old Fire Trail Road heading toward KY 90. Event time is radar estimated.
Trained spotter reported hail up to the size of quarters.
Corrects previous hail report from 1 E Dorthae. Trained spotter reported hail up to the size of quarters.
Public report. Time estimated from radar.
Report of tree down near the intersection of Fixer road and Hwy 11 north of Zoe. Report through social media.
Picture sent to use through social media.
A public report of half dollar sized hail with picture was received. The event time is radar estimated.

Multiple social media videos of hail falling near Stable Branch Road and KY-30. Lots of smaller hail scattered on the ground, but there are also multiple quarter-size stones visible in one of the videos. Time estimated via radar.
A tree was reported to have been blown down onto KY Route 30 near the Shoulderblade community. The event time is radar estimated.
An outbuilding, a fence, and trees were knocked down and damaged as large hail around a quarter in size was also reported off of Smith Branch Road between Ruby Dr and Copperhead Road.
Several trees were reported to be down along KY 476 about 3 miles from KY 15 with power out.
Quarter sized hail was reported about 3 miles on KY 476 from KY 15.
Corrects previous hail report from Decoy. Social media pictures of large, approximately ping pong ball sized hail in Decoy, KY. Time estimated via radar.

Social media photograph of various sized hail stones covering the ground in hippo. There are numerous large stones that stick out amongst the rest, and these are at least quarter-sized. Time estimated via radar.
Social media picture of measured quarter sized hail along Community Church road in Hueysville, KY. Time estimated via radar.
A public report from the Garrett community with a picture of hail at least quarter sized. The event time is radar estimated.
A public report from the Lackey community with a picture of hail around quarter sized. The event time is radar estimated.
Social media report of downed trees along Island Creek Road in Pike County. Time estimated via radar.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.). This is likely a delayed report and time of the event was based on radar.
Social media report of nickel to quarter sized hail in Loyall, KY between 12:35 and 12:40am.
Social media report of a downed tree along route 413 in Baxter, just to the north of Harlan. Time estimated via radar.
Social media report of quarter sized hail along Highway 413 in Baxter, just to the north of Harlan. Time estimated via radar.
Corrects hail report from Eolia. Social media picture of hail scattered on the ground in Eolia. Multiple stones of at least quarter-size visible. Time estimated via radar.
Social media picture of hail scattered on the ground in Eolia. Multiple stones of at least quarter-size visible. Time estimated via radar.
Montgomery County Dispatch reports widespread tree damage across the county from the storms this evening. Point placed at a time and place where radar velocity returns were at a local maxima.

Resending previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 SSW Ewington due to IRIS error message. Montgomery County Dispatch reports downed trees blocking Osborne Road from the thunderstorms earlier this evening. Time estimated via radar.
Trees were reported to have been blown down and a fence damaged at a residence along Oldham Road in the vicinity of Camargo Heights. The event time and location are both estimated.
Montgomery County Dispatch reports widespread tree damage across the county from the storms this evening. Point placed at a time and place where radar velocity returns were at a local maxima.
Trees and powerlines were reported to have been blown down in more than one location along Shrout Road area in the Kendall Springs community. The event time is radar estimated.
Trees and powerlines were reported to have been blown down in the Preston community. The event time is radar estimated.
Trees and powerlines were reported to have been blown down at 926 Old Flemingsburg Rd., Morehead. The event time is radar estimated.
Trees and powerlines were reported to have been blown down at 308 Clearfield Hill Road. The event time is radar estimated.
Trees and powerlines were reported to have been blown down at the intersection of Brown Street and Pettit Avenue. The event time is radar estimated.
Picture of power lines downed by a fallen tree in the South Irvine community. Time estimated via radar.
Jackson County 911 Center reported a tree down near the 580 Block of Brushy Ridge Rd. Time estimate from radar.
Social media report of a tree down on Privett Lane in the Wolverine area of Breathitt Count. Time estimated via radar.
2- to 3-inch diameter tree branch blocking the northbound lane of KY Highway 1812 approximately one-half mile north of the Breathitt County line. Time estimated from radar.
A tree was reported to have been downed and blocking Down and Around Road off of KY 30 and also south of Exit 70 along the Mountain Parkway. The event time is radar estimated.
Social media report of a tree down near the old Vincent Post Office, time estimated via radar.
Wayne County 911 reports trees downed on Dugan Street in Monticello. Time estimated via radar.
Social media report of a small tree down on a barn near the end of Freeman Fork Road in extreme SW Breathitt County. Time estimated via radar.
Social media reported quarter sized hail near Grassy Creek, Morgan County. Time estimated by radar.
Pulaski County 911 call center reported tree blown down at the intersection of Greenwood Lane and KY-1248, blocking half of roadway.
Tree blown down and blocking entire highway at around 1000 East Frog Hollow Road.
Tree partially blocking highway near the intersection of Mark Welborn Road and Barnesburg Road.
Media relayed photos of quarter sized hail that occurred south of Booneville, near the town of Taft. Time estimated by radar.
Media relayed photos of quarter size hail that occurred in the town of River. Time estimated by radar.
AWOS station K1A6 Middlesboro Apt.
Multiple trees reported down in the Arjay area including the 6900 block of KY-66 and around the Mose-Godsey curve.
Tree down in front of Heidelberg Baptist Church.
Tree reported down on Old Bell High Road.
Tree reportedly blown down at Williams Branch.
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd gst report from 1 N Keith. AWOS station KI35 Harlan Apt.
Lipfird Street bridge blocked by large tree.
Tractor trailer reportedly blown over on KY-38 near the Marathon gas station.
Picture of multiple hailstones compared with a penny NE of London. The largest stones are at least double the size of the penny. Time estimated via radar.
Trained spotter reported half dollar sized hail at the intersection of East Jellico Road and KY 92. Time estimated via radar.
Social media pictures of at least egg sized hail in Trosper around 4pm. The hail broke car windshields. Time estimated to be 4:03pm via radar.
Public report of 1.00 inch measured hail NW of Fourmile, time estimated via radar.
Public shared video of small hailstones, with larger quarter sized hail embedded. This occurred on Ky-225 in Trosper near the intersection of Number One Hollow Road. Time estimated by radar.

Social media video of lots of hail falling near the intersection of Hooppole Creek Road and Marydell Road in Eastern Laurel County around 4:15 PM. Time confirmed by radar. When the video pans to the ground, there are stones of various sizes visible. Some are at least quarter sized.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Hailstones ranging in size between 1.5 inches and 2.5 inches fell.
Delayed social media report of half dollar sized hail in Rockholds. Report passed on by SKYWARN HAM Network. Time estimated via radar.
Social media picture of golf ball sized hail on Jacks Fork Road off 26. Time estimated via radar.
Trained spotter reported estimated golf ball size hail. Time estimated from radar.
Large amounts of ping pong ball sized hail reported near the Doughboy statue in Downtown Monticello. Time estimated via radar.
Picture on social media of a quarter sized hail stone in Boons Camp. Time estimated via radar.

Pictures on social media of structure damage along Cherry Street in Monticello from the large hail that fell. Multiple broken windows and tattered vinyl siding visible. Piles of large, melting hailstones in the foreground. Time estimated via radar.

Multiple reports of broken windows and damaged vinyl siding in Monticello from the large hail this evening. At a residence along Bob Carrender Road, there were photos of holes in the siding. Hail size estimated to be at least ping pong ball. Time estimated via radar.
Pictures on social media of quarter to ping pong ball sized hail in Essie. Time estimated via radar.

Corrects time. Re-sends previous hail report from Bryants Store due to IRIS error message. Pictures on social media of ping pong ball sized hail from 5:15 pm in the Little Poplar Creek region of Bryants Store KY. Time confirmed via radar.
Multiple pictures of baseball sized hail in Monticello. One such stone was near the intersection of Hwy 3285/Hwy 776. Time estimated via radar.

Picture on social media of piles of melting large hail along Michigan Avenue in Monticello. Some of the storms are at least quarter sized still, and the entirety of the ground is covered. The road is covered in water from the hail melting, and vehicles are covered in tree debris after the hail finished falling.
Hail estimated at quarter size was reported to have fallen near the intersection of KY 1780 and US 421. The event time is radar estimated.

Social media picture of hail covering the ground north of Keavy along 192. Majority of the stones are closer to quarter sized, but a few significantly larger stones are visible, including some that are at least egg sized. Time specified by photographer and confirmed by radar.
Report from mPING: Golf Ball (1.75 in.). The event time is estimated from radar.
A social media report showed a picture of a couple hail stones estimated be the size of golf balls in Delta. Time estimated from radar.

Hail larger than quarter size was reported near Cutshin along KY 699 south of the 6 to 7 mile markers and hail of various sizes fell along the route to the Perry County line. The event time is radar estimated.

Corrects the previous hail report from 1 NW Davisburg. It was in the wrong spot. This report is now in the correct location, up KY 190 closer to Clear Creek Springs. Social media report of at least quarter sized hail breaking windshields along Log Mountain in Bell County just before 6pm.
Social media report of at least quarter sized hail breaking windshields along Log Mountain in Bell County around 6pm.

This is the previous hail report from 1 NW Davisburg. It is in the wrong spot. The actual report was up the road closer to Clear Creek Springs. Social media report of at least quarter sized hail breaking windshields along Log Mountain in Bell County around 6pm.
Trained spotter reports nickel to quarter sized hail north of Whitley City. Time confirmed via radar.
Social media picture of a 2 inch hail stone near Ky 700 and Jennys Branch Road NE of Whitley City. Time estimated via radar.
Social media picture of a few quarter sized hail stones in Barbourville amongst piles of smaller hail on the ground. Time provided was 6:06 pm and radar corroborates this.

Picture on social media of very large hail at mile marker 6 on Williamsburg St in McCreary County near Watters Ridge Rd. The hail is in someones hand and it is four fingers wide. Time provided by the individual who made the report and was confirmed with radar imagery.
Picture on social media of two very large hailstones near the intersection of E Williamsburg Street and State Highway 478. They are in a womans hand and are 3 fingers wide. Time estimated via radar.

Re-sends previous hail report from Bryants Store due to IRIS error message. Pictures on social media of ping pong ball sized hail from 6:15 pm in the Little Poplar Creek region of Bryants Store KY. Time confirmed via radar.
Pictures of snapped telephone poles near the 1900 Block of KY-225 were relayed from local HAM radio operators. Time estimated from radar.

Video on social media of lots of hail falling near the intersection of Highland Park Avenue and the Cumberland Falls Highway (US 25W) in Williamsburg. A few larger, approximately quarter-sized stones visible in the grass. Time estimated via radar.
Video on social media of large quantities of hail falling in Keaton. Quarter sized stones visible on the ground. Time estimated via radar.
Social media report of quarter sized hail along Browns Fork Road in Perry County around 6:35 pm. Time corroborated via radar.
Social media pictures of quarter sized hailstones in someones hand and videos of small hail piles in Gorman Hollow in Hazard. Time estimated via radar.
Multiple social media pictures of quarter sized hail near Woodland Park in Hazard. Time estimated via radar.
Social media report of measured quarter sized hail along Upper Second Creek Road in Hazard. Timestamp of 6:45pm, corroborated by radar.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Public posted photos of Ping Pong Ball sized hail near the city of Cordia. Time estimated by radar.
Social media picture of half dollar sized hail along Lotts Creek Road in Perry County. Time estimated via radar.
Social media picture of quarter to half dollar sized hail along South Lake Drive in Prestonsburg. Time provided by poster and corroborated via radar.

Picture on social media of hail in the community of Fisty. Hail was accumulating on the ground, with stones between quarter and half dollar size visible in the grass. Time provided by sender and corroborated via radar.
Social media report of quarter sized hail in Middlesboro at 7:10pm.
Social media video of large hailstones falling in Emma. Size estimated to be around golf ball. Time estimated via radar.
Corrects time of previous hail report from Boons Camp. Picture on social media of a quarter sized hail stone in Boons Camp. Time estimated via radar.

Cumberland Gap National Historic Park made a social media post that the Pinnacle Road and HWY 988 sections in the park are closed until further notice due to clearing of storm debris. Time estimated via radar.
Picture on social media of a measured 2.5 inch hail stone found at the intersection of KY-194 and KY-1428 between 7:10 and 7:15 pm.
A public report said 1 inch hail fell at the intersection Open Fork and Bent Branch Rd. Time estimated from radar.
Social media report of a tree down blocking the westbound lane of KY 476 between Flintville and Clayhole. Time estimated via radar.
Delayed social media report of quarter sized hail along Meathouse Road in Kimper. Time provided by sender, corroborated by radar.
Public and also Media shared photos out of McAndrews, KY showing 2 inch hail. Time estimated by radar.
Social media report of hail along Riley Branch road, near Flint Ridge Cemetery. Mostly smaller stones, but a couple of quarters mixed in. Time provided by reporter, corroborated via radar.
A picture of estimated golf ball sized hail was sent to the office from Belfry. Time estimated from radar.
Social media picture of a measured 1 inch hailstone along Upper Blackberry Road southwest of Ransom, KY. Time estimated via radar.
Picture on social media of half dollar sized hail in Pinsonfork, KY. Time estimated via radar.
Over 6 inches of flowing water near the 800 block of Main Street.
Over 6 inches of flowing water near the intersection of Madison Avenue and Broadway Street.
Social media photos of estimated 2 to 3 inch diameter hail from the Tyner Lake area of Jackson County.
Hail up to approximately egg size was reported along KY-290 between Annville and McKee. Event time estimated from radar.
Tree blown into the power lines near the intersection of Rocky Branch Road and Main Street. Event time estimated from radar.
Two trees blown down along Turkey Road near the intersection with Stepstone Road.
Nickel to quarter-sized hail observed at the Wendell H Ford Airport.
Emergency Manager reported golf ball-sized hail along Noble Branch.
Photo shared via social media of multiple hail stones of at least 2 inch diameter.
Photo via social media of 2.00 to 2.50-inch diameter hail. Time estimated from radar.
Picture on social media shows approximately quarter-sized hail near Wabd. Time of occurrence estimated from radar.
County dispatch reported a tree blown down and partially blocking KY-2030.
Picture on social media showed approximately egg-size hail. Time of occurrence estimated from radar.
Hail fell from 12:15 to 12:20 PM. Photo provided to the office.
Tree blocking road near 200 Old Bronston School Road.
Trees and power lines down along KY Highway 3106 and KY Highway 1275 N between Monticello and Steubenville. Exact location estimated.
Several trees down blocking Close Road.
Tree down on road.
Couple of trees down blocking the road near the intersection of Clay Hill Road and Kettlehead Road.
Time of occurrence estimated from radar.
Roof peeled off a business onto multiple adjacent vehicles. Vehicles have sustained unspecified damage.
Multiple photos of large tree snapped and split three ways and blocking the intersection of Whitley Street and Jody Street in London. Power pole snapped and power lines down due to snapped tree.
Photos of two hailstones with diameter greater than 2 inches. Largest stone appears to be 2.75 inches or larger.
A spotter in southeast London noted that trees were downed along Barrett Road as the storms moved through. Time estimated by radar.
Tree down and blocking Moores Creek School Road. Time estimated from radar.
A couple of trees down near the intersection of Sasser School Road and Shields Lane.

Report via the Pike County EM.
Several trees down in the Curry Branch Rd and KY Highway 80 area.

Trees down between the 400 and 500 block of Lick Branch Road.

Tree down on power line. Time estimated from radar. Possible that damage was caused by subsequent storm about 30 minutes later.

At least ten reports of trees down with power outages throughout the county and impacting most major thoroughfares, including US Highway 421 and KY Highway 80. Radar estimates strong winds from around 1:30 PM to approximately 2:50 PM based on radar.

State police reported a tree blown down near the Millstone community.

Floyd County Sherriff reported flash flooding over Salt Lick Road (KY-2029) around the 700 block. Road impassable.
Tree blown down and blocking Rawlins Road. Event time estimated from radar.

Pictures on social media show multiple trees blown down, including onto residence and vehicles along Fallen Road. Minor wind damage was also sustained to siding and roofing of a single wide trailer.
Tree reported blown down along CCC Trail. Event time estimated from radar.

County dispatch reported that many individual trees blown down around the county, but provided no specific details.

Martin County dispatch reported over a dozen instances of trees blown down onto or along highways around the county. Some of the trees involved power and phone lines. The Pilgrim, Warfield, and Inez areas may have had a larger concentration of the damage.
Many trees were reported to have been blown down along KY 2009 (Greasy Creek Road) between about MM 12 and MM 14. The event time is estimated from radar.
KY-7 closed by large tree blown down and blocking both lanes. Power lines were knock down. Event time estimated from radar.

County dispatch reported trees blown down in many parts of the county but did not provide specific details. KY-160 and KY-1103 apparently stood out as highways which saw a greater number of trees down. Time approximate.
Chloe Road was closed near Pikeville Elementary School due to water over the roadway.

Tree down at intersection of Mill and 5th streets. Time estimated off of radar.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 NE Hooker. Tree reported on a truck along with powerlines in the vicinity of 712 Ephram Creek Rd. Time estimated from radar.
Social media report of a large tree down near the mouth of Johnson Fork in the Hazel Green area of Wolfe County. Time estimated via radar.
Tree reported on a truck along with powerlines. Time of report - probably from an earlier storm.
Delayed report. Tree down on Route 229 around 4 miles south of the intersection between KY-1189 and KY-229 and in the vicinity of Camp Ground School. Time estimated via radar.

Corrects time and type of previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 SW Conway. After further clarification from the individual who reported the downed tree on social media, it was not caused from thunderstorm winds. Rather, runoff along a sloped hillside caused a tree to fall onto a vehicle and utility lines at the property below. Emergency crews were called out at approximately 9:30pm last night, and there were no nearby storms at that time.
Tree down on a vehicle and utility lines. Could have been from an earlier storm.
Tree down across road on Dunigan Branch Rd near Coal Branch Rd. Exact location estimated. Time estimated from radar.
Downed tree on a home along Radio Hill Road in Manchester. Time estimated via radar. Report came from social media and was relayed to us via Slack.
Downed trees in the 1100 block of Left Fork Little Paint Road. Time estimated via radar.
Trees reported down along North Central Avenue behind the Wendys in Prestonsburg. Time estimated via radar.
Trees reported down in the 500 Block of KY-1428 east of Prestonsburg. Time estimated via radar.

Delayed report. Pictures on social media from RC Arnett Road show snapped electric pole with power lines and transformer on the ground. Extensive roofing material blown off of a home. Multiple large trees and branches snapped. Event time estimated from radar.

Delayed report. Multiple reports on social media of branches and and a large tree blown down in the vicinities of Jim Arnett and Birch Branch roads. Pictures show Burning Fork Road mostly block by downed trees. Event time estimated from radar.
Delayed report. Picture on social media shows tree blown down along Mullins Road. Nearby vegetable garden severely windblown. Time estimated from radar.
Video on social media of flash flooding along Big Card Road in Mouthcard, KY. Muddy water is flowing over the road. Video was posted at 2:27pm.
Delayed social media report of flash flooding near the intersection of Trace Fork Road and Left Fork Trace Fork. Pictures show muddy water flowing over the road.
Social media report of the creek covering the road in three separate places along Little Card Road.
A social media report said a porch roof was peeled halfway off on the 100 block of Bethany School Lane. Time estimated from radar.

Knox County 911 dispatch reported emergency crews were deployed to KY-1527 due to reports of water on the road. Public reports on social media corroborate this, with one member of the public estimating between 4 and 8 inches of water running across the road.

Updates previous flash flood report from 1 ESE Garrich with new information. Knox County 911 dispatch reported emergency crews were deployed to KY-1527 due to reports of water on the road. Public reports on social media corroborate this, with one member of the public estimating between 4 and 8 inches of water running across the road near the intersection with R P Bargo Loop. Local media later reported that there was a water rescue at this intersection during the peak of the flash flooding.
HAM Radio Operator passed on a report of a large downed tree at 10:15 PM in Allen, Floyd County.
Flash flooding washed out Camp Creek Road in between the intersection of Tidwell Road and Paul Howard Cemetery Road. Time was estimated from radar.
Tree down near the intersection of Morehead Road and James Road.
Water rescue at a residence near the head of Crane Creek Road. Water did inundate the residence.
A public report via Twitter of flood waters surrounding a home along KY 26 near Dollar General.
A video of flood waters up to a residence along Eatontown Road near Woodbine was received via social media.
Intersection of KY 6 with KY 1064 at the Woodbine Fire department is completely under water and impassible.
Picture on social media of water covering US-25 near the Brindle Ridge Volunteer Fire Department in Conway.

Rockcastle County 911 received a report of flowing water with debris over S Upper Ridge Road at 3:28 PM. The estimated location of the report corroborates with traffic slow-downs on various mapping platforms.

Owsley County 24 Hour Warning Point reported flash flooding at the end of Chadwell Road from Little Sturgeon Creek. The flooding washed out a culvert and damaged the road. Time of report provided as 4:30pm.
An off-duty NWS Employee reported water over the road in multiple places along Spencer Creek Road east of Mount Sterling.
Delayed social media report of flash flooding along Prestonsburg Street in West Liberty from 7/20/25. Video shows flowing water from Long Branch over the road near a hardware store.

Corrects the location of the previous flash flood report. The picture mentioned below was actually taken at the intersection of Rayburn Fork Road and US-60. The correct location is in Carter County. Social media report of water flowing over US-60 in several places between Morehead and Olive Hill. A picture of one of these instances was provided from near the Poppy Mountain Dollar general around 6:30pm. There is a barn in the background.

Social media report of water flowing over US-60 in several places between Morehead and Olive Hill. A picture of one of these instances was provided from near the Poppy Mountain Dollar general around 6:30pm.
Delayed social media report of flash flooding near the intersection of CR-1322 and Yocum Road on 7/20/25. Flowing water from Pleasant Run covered the road.

Delayed social media report of flash flooding along Centerville Road in Morgan County on 7/20/25. Pictures show the intersection with Church Lane under flowing water from Little Caney Creek. Access to Centerville Community Church was temporarily cut off.
Morgan County Sherriff posted a video of flash flooding in the 1500 Block of Main Street in West Liberty. Water is flowing over the road, and emergency crews are on the scene.
Off-duty NWS Employee reports that Blackwater Creek is out of its banks and flowing over the road. Vehicles are reportedly struggling to pass due to the running water.
Delayed report. Social media post with photos of KY Highway 7 closed by tree debris, mud, and rushing water across the road.

Delayed social media report of an earlier, bigger slide in the same location as the report from 9:15pm on 7/21. The sun is out in these pictures, and there are more trees/mud/debris visible blocking the road than there are in the second report around dusk. Water was likely flowing down the hill from a small tributary of the Right Fork Beaver Creek. Both streams appeared agitated in the pictures/videos, and additional flash flooding was likely ongoing.

Delayed social media report of a debris flow along KY-7 between Topmost and Wayland. Video on social media of running water across the road. The running water brought small trees down with it from the hillside, which blocked the road. A truck was unable to pass. Water was likely flowing down the hill from a small tributary of the Right Fork Beaver Creek. Both streams appeared agitated in the pictures/videos, so additional flash flooding was likely ongoing. Time estimated from the person who posted video on social media.
Corrects previous report from 2 NNE Mchargue. several trees down near the Campground Elementary School and Campground Fire Department off of KY229.
several trees down at the fire camp near KY229.
A few rescues as water has encroached homes in the area off of KY 229. Also numerous roads closed per county dispatch.
Estill County Dispatch reports multiple trees down along White Oak Road east of KY-89 north of Irvine. Time provided by dispatch.
Lee County 911 received a report of a large downed tree along Pine Grove Road east of KY-11 South. Time estimated via radar.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 N Saul. Perry County 911 reports downed power lines in the 300 Block of Abner Fork Road from the thunderstorm that moved through the Buckhorn area earlier this evening. Time estimated via radar.
Perry County 911 reports downed power lines on Abner Branch Road from the thunderstorm that moved through the Buckhorn area earlier this evening. Time estimated via radar.
Perry County 911 reports downed power lines along KY 451 near Meteor Lane from the thunderstorm that moved through the Krypton area earlier this evening. Time provided by dispatch.
Knox County dispatch reports a downed tree on KY 3441 from the thunderstorms earlier this evening.
Knox County Dispatch received a report of downed trees off KY-6 and KY-459 near Gray Lane from a thunderstorm at 7:30pm.
Pictures on social media show several inches of rapidly flowing water washing debris down Smith Creek Road in the 1700 block.
The Leslie County Emergency Operations Center reported a downed tree just to the east of KY-80 in the Wooton community. Time estimated via radar.
Picture on social media of downed trees blocking Fairview Road in Middlesboro between Mudlick Road and Gordon Lane. Time estimated via radar.

The Middlesboro Police Department posted pictures of a large tree down on Balmoral Road in between Pike Road and Rochester Avenue. The tree fell on power lines, and additional social media videos from this location showed a small fire on the ground from these downed lines. Balmoral road was temporarily closed due to this damage. There were also scattered reports of power outages in Middlesboro from this storm, including in the Balmoral/Alpine Road neighborhood. Exact time estimated via radar.
Picture on social media of a large downed tree blocking KY-1092 between Flatgap and Drake Fork Road. Time estimated via radar.
Tree down on power line at the intersection of KY Hwy 11 and KY Highway 715.
Martin County dispatch reports a downed tree blocking the road at the intersection of Rock House Road and Lower Wolf Branch Road. Time estimated via radar. Stronger-worded SPS was in effect.

Wolfe County dispatch received a delayed report this morning of downed trees in a holler blocking Blue Balk Road. Multiple storms moved through this area, but the strongest velocity data at this particular location came with the first storm of the evening. Thus, the time was estimated to be 6:45pm via radar.
Lee County 911 relayed a report of a downed tree at mile marker 4 from the storms that occurred before 8pm last night. Time estimated via radar.
Martin County Dispatch reported a downed tree blocking the road in the 6300 block of Beauty Road/KY-40. Time estimated via radar.

Magoffin County Dispatch reports a tree blocking South River Road near Owens Cemetery and Oakley Creek. The tree was down on the stretch of River Road that parallels the Licking River before the intersection with KY-1635 and the bridge that crosses the river. Time estimated via radar.
Multiple pictures on social media of measured quarter-sized hail in Shopville, KY. Time estimated via radar.
Magoffin County Dispatch reports a downed tree blocking May Branch Road just off of Highway 460. Time estimated via radar.

Video on social media of hail coming down near the intersection of Walton Road and Nolan Road. Largest stones appear to be quarter sized, with more widespread smaller hailstones. Time provided by poster and corroborated with radar.
Delayed report. Large tree blown down by thunderstorm winds between a house and a car, causing a scratch to the car. Time estimated via radar.
Lee County 911 relayed a report of downed trees along Fairgrounds Ridge Road near the Three Forks Regional Jail. Time estimated via radar.
Lee County 911 relayed a report of a downed tree in the 200 Block of Gilliam Road. Time estimated via radar.

Social media report of downed trees one mile south of the Wolfe/Breathitt County Line on KY-15. Report corroborated with a traffic slowdown on online GPS software. Time estimated via radar.

Social media report of a downed tree at the Vancleve Park and Ride immediately after the exit ramp from KY-15. Time estimated via radar.

Magoffin County Dispatch received a delayed report earlier this morning of large tree branches downed and blocking Lakeville Road near the intersection with Bear Branch Road and River Road. This damage occurred from the storms around midnight. Exact time estimated via radar.
A tree and powerlines down blocking the right lane of KY-39 at Petrey Road. The event time is radar estimated.
At KY-70 and Estill Hackney Road a tree was reported to be down blocking all lanes of the roadway. The event time is radar estimated.

Several tree limbs and tree debris reported to have been blown down onto KY Route 7 near the Morgan County line. The event time is radar estimated.

Delayed report. The Fleming Mason utility company had made a social media post that they could not access power outages in the vicinity of Anderson Branch and Muses Mill Roads to complete repairs after sunrise. After following up with their public affairs division, they confirmed that this was due to high water blocking the roads entering this area that morning. The time of the flooding LSR was estimated using the peak of the archived FLASH CREST Unit Streamflow data during the event.
Large tree down on Hwy 2392. Reported around 2 pm.
Six downed trees along a seven mile stretch of roadway along KY 1524 took out power and cable lines, in the Goose Rock area. Time estimated from radar.
Trees were reported down on Possum Hollow road, time estimated from radar.
Reports of tree and power line damage all up and down KY 223 and KY 718. Time estimated from radar.
Tree damage was reported at the corner of 223 and Moore Creek Road.
Trees reported down on AJ Gray Cemetery Road, near Flat Lick. Time estimated from radar.
Tree was reported blown down and blocking a lane of KY-15 near Jeff Mart. Event time estimated from radar.
A tree was reported down near the Racoon community along Frozen Creek Court. Time was estimated from radar.
Down trees and powerlines were reported along East Big Creek Road, just off Highway 119 outside the Huddy community. Time was estimated from radar.
Law enforcement reported a tree down on Lick Mountain Road in Lick Creek. Time was estimated from radar.
Little Poplar Creek Volunteer Fire Department posted that they responded to downed trees blocking the roadway near the intersection of KY-11 South and KY-1530. Time estimated via radar.

Delayed social media report of structural damage from thunderstorm wind gusts the night of December 18th. Pictures show a metal building in the 4300 Block of KY-7 with a peeled back roof. Pieces of sheet metal were blown straight into the yard of the neighboring property, along with lawn ornaments, outdoor furniture, and a metal swing set. Time estimated via radar.
Letcher County EM shared pictures of downed trees and power lines along KY-7 in between Adams Branch Rd and County Rd 1339.

*** 1 INJ *** Large tree limb fell through roof of trailer house. Female lying in bed was struck causing a deep laceration on leg. Injured female was transported by ambulance to local hospital. County EM relocated family to safe place.
Mesonet station DORT Pikeville 13 S.

Social media post with security camera footage shows the metal roof of a porch blown off and then across a yard. Footage time stamp shows time of damage as 12:26 AM, and this is corroborated with radar data.
